```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 503} id="spcbF_1lgIAB" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680787111952, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 720, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="2fb0cbd3-8dca-44df-f63a-b30d3e208033"

modeleReg.fit(X,y)

print("intercept :",modeleReg.intercept_)
print("coef :", modeleReg.coef_)

#calcul du R²
modeleReg.score(X,y)
print("R² :", modeleReg.score(X,y))
RMSE=np.sqrt(((y-modeleReg.predict(X))**2).sum()/len(y))
print("RMSE :", RMSE)

plt.scatter(X.iloc[:,0], y)
# Plot regression line
xseq = np.linspace(X.iloc[:,0].min(), X.iloc[:,0].max(), num=100)
plt.plot(xseq, modeleReg.intercept_ + modeleReg.coef_[0] * xseq,  color="red", lw=2.5)
plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="8pJLHMo-ZLbt" -->
###**Analyse de la Variance Multiples (MANOVA)**
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="KSREo1IG5szv" -->
La procédure Analyse multivariée de variance (MANOVA) fournit une analyse de régression et une analyse de variance pour plusieurs variables dépendantes par une ou plusieurs variables de facteur ou covariables. Les facteurs divisent la population en groupes.

La MANOVA permet de tester l'égalité des vecteurs de moyennes dans les différentes populations parentes. Autrement dit, notons la moyenne de la variable Xk dans le groupe j ; l'hypothèse nulle s'écrit alors : tandis que l'hypothèse alternative est la négation de H0 (test bilatéral).
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="cIQRDl99ZORx"
from statsmodels.multivariate.manova import MANOVA
```

```python id="lbE63qIUZTbl"
maov = MANOVA.from_formula('sepal_length + sepal_width + petal_length + petal_width  ~ species', data=iris)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="z2uUXAJ6ZqWx" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680857066380, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 12, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="9cc11ffa-c780-414a-b09e-eb421c6a2bbe"
print(maov.mv_test())
```

<!-- #region id="4jS1VBkSElFf" -->
### **Analyse en Composantes Principales (PCA)**
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="TCY8rRwKvozQ" -->
#### **Sklearn**
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="7R3Z-m_RePvM" -->
* **Import des librairy**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="QQkUkz52eyuT"
from itertools import chain
import sys
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
#classe pour standardisation
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
#classe pour l'ACP
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
import sklearn.decomposition

```

```python id="e4L945FSbOU_"
# Fit Transforme

def DataFrame_to_PCA(df,components):
    """
    Selectionne les varaibles qualitatives d'un dataFrame et applique une décomposition en N composante principales passé en paramètre.
    Retourne le model PCA , les données transformé et la liste des nom des colonnes.
    """
    df = df.select_dtypes(exclude=['object','bool','category','datetime64','timedelta'])
    Columns = list(df.columns)
    # On replace nos valeurs manquante par une valuer arbitraire.

    imputer = SimpleImputer( missing_values=np.nan , strategy='mean' )
    X = imputer.fit_transform(df.values)

    # On centre et reduit nos variable quantitatives
    X =  StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)

    if components == 0:
        components = df.shape[1]-1

    pca = PCA(n_components=components)

    pca.fit(X)
    return pca, X, Columns
```

```python id="YMkkIohSnndd"
compo = 3
pca, X, varNames = DataFrame_to_PCA(iris, compo)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="K-0USVST2HmH" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634712040729, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 17, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="09a449d2-24d5-420d-e062-fd250ba83a63"
X.shape
```

```python id="Xa8cvk530mRm"
def Variance_Exp(pca):
    """
    Retourne tableau du % de variance expliqué pour les N Axes selectionné
    """
    n = len(pca.explained_variance_ratio_)
    eig = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "Dim" : ["Dim " + str(x + 1) for x in range(n)],
            "Val prop" : np.round(pca.explained_variance_ , decimals=2),
            "% var expli" : np.round(pca.explained_variance_ratio_ * 100 , decimals=2),
            "Sum % var" : np.round(np.cumsum(pca.explained_variance_ratio_ * 100) , decimals=2 )
        },
        columns = ["Dim", "Val prop", "% var expli", "Sum % var"]
    )
    return eig
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="TPrx9aqzepd4" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634712040731, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 16, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="1b3ab79b-fe08-4ef9-cb28-27b49baf621b"
eigs = Variance_Exp(pca)
print(eigs)
```

```python id="1XRXbmk43tOS"
 def Contrib_Var(pca ,X , varNames , compo ):
    """
    fonction permettant de visualiszer les variables les plus contributrices, selon les axes de chacunes de composantes de la PCA
    """

    # construction de notre matrice identite
    iden = np.identity(X.shape[1])

    #transformation sur les variables
    contrib_var = pca.transform(iden)

    # Contruction du dtaframe à partir de notre matrice transformé
    All_contribs = pd.DataFrame(np.round(contrib_var,3), columns =  ["Dim "+str(i) for i in  range(1, compo+1) ]  , index = varNames)

    Cols = list(chain.from_iterable(("Var D"+str(i), "Dim "+str(i) ) for i in range(1, compo+1)))
    Contribs = pd.DataFrame(columns=Cols)

    for i , col in zip( range(1,compo+1) , All_contribs.columns):
        temp = All_contribs[[col]]
        temp = temp.sort_values(by=col, ascending=False)
        Contribs["Var D"+str(i)] = list(temp.index)
        Contribs["Dim "+str(i)] = list(temp[col])

    return All_contribs ,Contribs
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="KHzvRUV2fIqU" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634712100409, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 5, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="17a42fdd-77b7-44ae-d631-c5f6177cd963"
df_contrib ,Contrib = Contrib_Var(pca ,X , varNames , compo)
print(Contrib)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="slaJ0jfWfbgL" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634713082551, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 305, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="ab9f7e90-7454-4918-b4d2-8e3f66d2d34e"
print(X.shape , pca.transform(X).shape)
```

```python id="2GCzfcq404AM"
 def Contrib_indiv(pca ,X , y , compo , Yname):
    Xt =  pd.DataFrame(pca.transform(X).round(decimals=3) , columns=["Dim " + str(x) for x in range(1,compo+1) ])
    if y != None:
        Xt[Yname] = y

    return Xt
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 206} id="HQF69wAJfske" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634713332644, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 3, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="12dbac3e-b34e-40f5-a132-411c21e84485"
Xt = Contrib_indiv(pca ,X , y , compo , 'espece')
Xt.head()
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="xrMl8Uk1imD3" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634714516371, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 217, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="78df090b-8f9f-43d6-ace8-17a56ca47702"
Xt.iloc[: , -1].unique()
```

```python id="IESa8nn7haLI"
def plot_pca( Contrib_indiv , Axe1 , Axe2 ):
    fig = plt.figure(figsize = (8,8))
    ax = fig.add_subplot(1,1,1)
    ax.set_xlabel('Composante Principale '+str(Axe1), fontsize = 15)
    ax.set_ylabel('Composante Principale '+str(Axe2), fontsize = 15)
    ax.set_title('Reprsentation sur 2 Axes de la PCA', fontsize = 20)

    targets = list(Contrib_indiv.iloc[: , -1].unique()) # on recupère les valeur unique de la colonne contenant les label à afficher
    colors = ['blue' ,'orange' ,'green' ,'red' ,'purpule' ,'brown' ,'pink' ,'gray' ,'olive' ,'cyan' ,'k' ,'b' ,'g' ,'m']
    colors = colors[0:len(targets)]

    for target, color in zip(targets,colors):
        indicesToKeep = Contrib_indiv.iloc[: , -1] == target
        ax.scatter(Contrib_indiv.loc[indicesToKeep, 'Dim '+str(Axe1)],
                   Contrib_indiv.loc[indicesToKeep, 'Dim '+str(Axe2)],
                   c = color,
                   s = 50)
    ax.legend(targets)
    ax.grid()
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 523} id="dEpUP8sUksq1" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634715338708, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 707, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="320a5061-ce64-47ee-a63a-04c1692d4283"
plot_pca( Xt , 1 , 2 )
```

```python id="miJF8oo9pO4X"
import pandas as pd
import prince
from sklearn import datasets

iris  = sns.load_dataset("iris")
X = iris.drop(['species'], axis=1).to_numpy()
y = iris.species.to_numpy()

```

```python id="imAVnc9mpQJX"
pca = prince.PCA(
    n_components=2,
    n_iter=3,
    rescale_with_mean=True,
    rescale_with_std=True,
    copy=True,
    check_input=True,
    engine='auto',
    random_state=42
)
pca = pca.fit(X)
```

```python id="sr1C6IReycDA"
from fanalysis.pca import PCA
```

```python id="erssYcpiw3p8"
iris  = sns.load_dataset("iris")
X = iris.drop(['species'], axis=1).to_numpy()
y = iris.species.to_numpy()
```

```

<!-- #region id="3zzR_rcayzjh" -->
* pca.row\_contrib\_ : contributions des points lignes à la variance de l'axe
* pca.col\_contrib\_ : contributions des points colonnes à la variance de l'axe
* pca.row\_cos2\_ : cosinus carrés des points lignes
* pca.col\_cos2\_ : cosinus carrés des points colonnes

<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="iKKXtp5QzBzN"
my_pca.row_contrib_
pca.col_contrib_
pca.row_cos2
pca.col_cos2_
```

<!-- #region id="2BHrbMllJbOz" -->
### **Analyse Factorielle des Correspondances (CA)**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 175} id="F8wAcc0i4U9Z" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634721680702, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 221, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="320629fa-ad6f-478b-865d-f4af4614200a"
import pandas as pd

pd.set_option('display.float_format', lambda x: '{:.6f}'.format(x))
df = pd.DataFrame(
   data=[
       [326, 38, 241, 110, 3],
       [688, 116, 584, 188, 4],
       [343, 84, 909, 412, 26],
       [98, 48, 403, 681, 85]
   ],
   columns=pd.Series(['Fair', 'Red', 'Medium', 'Dark', 'Black']),
   index=pd.Series(['Blue', 'Light', 'Medium', 'Dark'])
)
df
```

<!-- #region id="Jy9M3BxO4YF5" -->
#### Fanalysis
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="osHrs3MH4IZS"
from fanalysis.ca import CA
```

```python id="sJLjDWNrNEMn"
X = df.to_numpy()
```

```python id="G95ZgjeaaN9R" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634719983294, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 217, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="35b75d54-e236-4b5f-95ab-15b14659d75f"
my_ca = CA(row_labels=df.index.values, col_labels=df.columns.values)
my_ca.fit(X)
```

<!-- #region id="O9Rs5I9H6N6u" -->
* my_ca.eig\_ : valeurs propres
* my_ca.row\_coord\_ : coordonnées des points lignes
* my_ca.col\_coord\_ : coordonnées des points colonnes
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="hFkhOiYbbgFG" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634720091183, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 235, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="276a6ad3-9a36-4b35-9c37-37ad65357994"
#    en 1ère ligne : les valeurs propres en valeur absolue
#    en 2ème ligne : les valeurs propres en pourcentage de la variance totale
#    en 3ème ligne : les valeurs propres en pourcentage cumulé de la variance totale
my_ca.eig_
```

```python id="FFvc--RxnUHx" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 244} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634720109890, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 724, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="fdcfa59d-33c4-4342-87fe-0dcb32353945"
labels = ['val abs','% var totale','% var tt cumule']
ticks = ["Axe_" + str(x) for x in range(1,len(my_ca.eig_[0])+1 ) ]


dat = pd.DataFrame.from_records(my_ca.eig_.T, columns=labels).astype(float)
axes = dat.plot(kind='bar',rot=0,lw=2,figsize=(30,4) ,
                title='Valeurs Propres', subplots=True, layout=(1,3))

plt.xticks(range(0,len(my_ca.eig_[0])), ticks)
plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="mUHcJquI7W7q" -->

<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="D7YzcVsGK48j"
"""#all in one
my_ca = CA(stats=False)
# Estimation du modèle
my_ca.fit(X)
# Projection de l'ensemble des points lignes sur les axes factoriels
my_ca.transform(X)
# both in time
my_ca.fit_transform(X)
# Projection d'une ligne supplémentaire
my_ca.transform([[25, 54, 41, 11, 65, 44]])"""

<!-- #region id="L029njVT_xBy" -->
#### Prince
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="QXHrY2Hm_zRl"
import prince

X=df

ca = prince.CA(
    n_components=2,
    n_iter=3,
    copy=True,
    check_input=True,
    engine='auto',
    random_state=42
)
X.columns.rename('Hair color', inplace=True)
X.index.rename('Eye color', inplace=True)
ca = ca.fit(X)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="kZlzLpl6B0wB" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634722792472, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 298, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="379448d8-5343-4253-f6e6-358533bc1a13"
print('Valeur propre', ca.eigenvalues_,'\n',
      'Pourcentage inertie expliqué',ca.explained_inertia_,'\n',
      'Pourcentage inertie total',ca.total_inertia_
)
```

<!-- #region id="rYQPAuADBPJj" -->
 * **Profils ligne**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 175} id="_EHp9g-QAtYq" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634721783110, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 276, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="3b59811b-180a-446d-de0b-c2770294262d"
ca.row_coordinates(X)
```

<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 206} id="nW_-3dq3AvTm" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634721791172, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 246, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="57afcb08-770d-43b6-8923-0ef668a36668"
ca.column_coordinates(X)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 791} id="UOT6gSsdBrKD" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634722040985, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 1122, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="19ee6360-7a72-470d-ba4b-0c7476b568ef"
ax = ca.plot_coordinates(
    X=X,
    ax=None,
    figsize=(6, 6),
    x_component=0,
    y_component=1,
    show_row_labels=True,
    show_col_labels=True
)
ax.get_figure()#.savefig('images/ca_coordinates.svg')
```

<!-- #region id="0EACzIr76fRG" -->
###**Analyse factorielle Multiples (MFA)**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="hVFGu1thK4_I" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 206} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634740735060, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 181, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="3f3f2213-21b9-4eca-8241-97248098d444"
 X = pd.DataFrame(
     data=[
         [1, 6, 7, 2, 5, 7, 6, 3, 6, 7],
         [5, 3, 2, 4, 4, 4, 2, 4, 4, 3],
         [6, 1, 1, 5, 2, 1, 1, 7, 1, 1],
         [7, 1, 2, 7, 2, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2],
         [2, 5, 4, 3, 5, 6, 5, 2, 6, 6],
         [3, 4, 4, 3, 5, 4, 5, 1, 7, 5]
     ],
     columns=['E1 fruity', 'E1 woody', 'E1 coffee',
              'E2 red fruit', 'E2 roasted', 'E2 vanillin', 'E2 woody',
              'E3 fruity', 'E3 butter', 'E3 woody'],
     index=['Wine {}'.format(i+1) for i in range(6)]
 )
 X['Oak type'] = [1, 2, 2, 2, 1, 1]
 X.head()
```

<!-- #region id="jZUvfxgjFIhb" -->
#### Prince
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="7vnnVtEZrvH9" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634740737284, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 176, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="33dcae1c-69e4-4dc5-85c0-dae58eb6552c"
groups = {
   'Expert #{}'.format(no+1): [c for c in X.columns if c.startswith('E{}'.format(no+1))]
   for no in range(3)
}

groups
```

```python id="I4FTFFVu_lMr"
import prince
# L'AMF hérite de la classe PCA, ce qui implique que vous avez accès à toutes ses méthodes et propriétés.

mfa = prince.MFA(
    groups=groups,
    n_components=12,
    n_iter=3,
    copy=True,
    check_input=True,
    engine='auto',
    random_state=42
)
mfa = mfa.fit(X)
```

```python id="-ycGMC58A9AH" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634740739120, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 194, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="ab6defbb-6144-453e-abeb-c1ee18ac46f0"
print("Inertie total du model: \n",mfa.total_inertia_,'\n')
print("Valeurs Propres associées aux Dimensions: \n",mfa.eigenvalues_,'\n')
print("% d'inertie expliqué pour chaque Dimension: \n",[x * 100 for x in mfa.explained_inertia_],'\n')
```

```python id="AcUg7AmiA7uJ" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634740740303, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 370, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="8ba3a53d-e99e-4b1b-864b-37a1cdffba83"
# Informations relatives à l'analyse factorielle (pour chaque groupe)
for name, fa in sorted(mfa.partial_factor_analysis_.items()):
    print('{} eigenvalues: {}'.format(name, fa.eigenvalues_))
```

<!-- #region id="eC1u7a1HDc8t" -->
* Individus:

<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="q7rXvS8N_tzz" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 206} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634740742249, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 295, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="bcd7caef-cb2b-4e67-b661-b1d425d90ceb"
# La méthode row_coordinates renvoie les coordonnées globales de chaque individus.
row_coordinates = mfa.row_coordinates(X)
row_coordinates.head()
```

```python id="KGgDt_rt_t5w" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 393} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634740743660, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 222, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="34a3a33b-b1f5-4a92-f9f6-519c89740017"
""" les coordonnées des lignes à l'intérieur de chaque groupe.
    La méthode partial_row_coordinates renvoie un DataFrame avec MultiIndex.
    Le premier niveau d'indexation correspond à chaque groupe spécifié,
    tandis que le niveau imbriqué indique les coordonnées à l'intérieur de chaque groupe.
"""
mfa.partial_row_coordinates(X)
```

```python id="2s8RazJJM-ys" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 363} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634733428643, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 189, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="f2c43183-d5b3-415e-bf44-90a0aaba59e7"

```python id="5NbFnnJ2D_sz" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 718} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634733886982, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 637, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="eb373007-0142-48b0-9a4d-8ac5c35f519d"
plt.figure(figsize=(20, 20))
y = list(correlation[0])
z = list(correlation[1])
n = list(correlation.index)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 12)  )
ax.scatter(z, y)

for i, txt in enumerate(n):
    ax.annotate(txt, (z[i], y[i]))
plt.show()
```

```python id="gQVZ2HxeD_vI" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 267} executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634736074531, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 585, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="d498bd37-722a-4748-b837-cb72760fdaee"
import numpy as np
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

reverse_groupe = {value: key for key in groups for value in groups[key]}
reverse_groupe
liste_lab = list(reverse_groupe.values())
liste_lab

scatter_x =  list(correlation[0])
scatter_y =  list(correlation[1])
group = liste_lab
cdict = {'Expert #1': 'red', 'Expert #2': 'blue', 'Expert #3': 'green'}

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
for g in np.unique(group):
    ix = np.where(np.array(group) == g)[0].tolist()
    x_sca = [scatter_x[index] for index in ix]
    y_sca = [scatter_y[index] for index in ix]
    ax.scatter(x_sca, y_sca, c = cdict[g], label = g, s = 100)
ax.legend()
plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="GRSmUcyFZKRj" -->
### **Analyse des Correspondances Multiples (MCA)**
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="4g6CiKY14p-D" -->
* Réaliser une Analyse des Correspondances Multiples pour expliquer:
  la marge, le nombre et le typed actions vétérinaire effectuées.

* C'est Une approche "datamining" : l'ACM vise à décrire la variabilité du jeu de données
afin de regrouper les modalites par "interet".
Cette approche est utilisé comme méthode de réduction des données,
le résultat peut servir d'entrée pour un modèle prédictif à effectuer plus tard
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="i5-euG0g-gA4" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634738050505, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 198, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="b9da9ee7-e1a8-482e-acdb-4ec4fdd9f9cb"
from google.colab import drive
drive.mount('/content/drive')
path = "/content/drive/MyDrive/Machine_Learning_Cheat_Sheet/Fanalysis/"
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 237} id="U4zSwzL3-gER" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634738854666, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 185, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="487d706c-eb2b-45c9-f913-d9f2dd439fa4"
df = pd.read_table(path+"mca_data_string.txt", header=0, index_col=0, delimiter="\t", encoding="utf-8")
df.head()
```

<!-- #region id="s0WtwxUnB6e9" -->
#### **Fanalysis**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="KYTs5wcsB6qR"
from fanalysis.mca import MCA
```

```python id="enyZjMLJBri2"
X = df.iloc[:, 0:6].to_numpy()
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="vsiP_lD_Bw94" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634738929524, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 202, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="b5ef60d6-332c-4414-e798-75b4b1ea3fb8"
my_mca = MCA(row_labels=df.index.values, var_labels=df.columns.values[0:6])
my_mca.fit(X)
```

<!-- #region id="r7ejnlqiCRLw" -->
*    my_pca.eig\_ : valeurs propres
*    my_pca.row\_coord\_ : coordonnées des points lignes
*    my_pca.col\_coord\_ : coordonnées des points colonnes
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="kGuV8qdKCYr2" -->
my_mca.eig_ contient :

    en 1ère ligne : les valeurs propres en valeur absolue
    en 2ème ligne : les valeurs propres en pourcentage de la variance totale
    en 3ème ligne : les valeurs propres en pourcentage cumulé de la variance totale

<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="KWI34OtHCMAW" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634738974330, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 307, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="b6c0b479-608f-467f-9f17-5f12df50dab8"
print(my_mca.eig_)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 246} id="1TQE1gyUCMC5" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739156934, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 931, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="cc5da1bb-fa3b-4f8f-a76e-302d4b0b3b2f"
labels = ['val Propre','% varience ','% variance cumule']
ticks = ["Axe_" + str(x) for x in range(1,len(my_mca.eig_[0])+1 ) ]

dat = pd.DataFrame.from_records(my_mca.eig_.T, columns=labels).astype(float)
axes = dat.plot(kind='bar',rot=0,lw=2,figsize=(30,4) ,
                title='Récapitulatif', subplots=True, layout=(1,3))

plt.xticks(range(0,len(my_mca.eig_[0])), ticks)
plt.show()
```

```python id="y_VkhodJCMFc"
"""my_mca.plot_eigenvalues()
my_mca.plot_eigenvalues(type="percentage")
my_mca.plot_eigenvalues(type="cumulative")"""
```

<!-- #region id="jdqlglL_DS4O" -->
* my_mca.row_contrib_ : contributions des points lignes à la variance de l'axe
* my_mca.col_contrib_ : contributions des points colonnes à la variance de l'axe
* my_mca.row_cos2_ : cosinus carrés des points lignes
* my_mca.col_cos2_ : cosinus carrés des points colonnes

<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 226} id="FUslRDgPCMH1" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739306561, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 221, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="1a704931-39fb-49cf-94ea-ba79a85f32d5"
df_rows = my_mca.row_topandas()
df_rows.head()
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 571} id="4HhlPFCRCMKL" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739344786, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 241, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="a4bfb014-e092-4073-c516-5c8d18186bbf"
df_cols = my_mca.col_topandas()
df_cols
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 458} id="OGQNSjiTD0Ob" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739421399, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 767, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="0ca5734e-a85a-4863-8a5f-9bc71664e101"
# Mapping simultané des points lignes et colonnes
# Les paramètres de la méthode mapping indiquent que ce sont les axes 1 et 2 qui sont ici représentés
my_mca.mapping(num_x_axis=1, num_y_axis=2, figsize=(8, 7))
```

```python id="r942nRuyD0Ra"
# Mapping des points lignes
my_mca.mapping_row(num_x_axis=1, num_y_axis=2, figsize=(8, 7))
```

```python id="uC6CVrPeD0T7"
# Mapping des points colonnes
my_mca.mapping_col(num_x_axis=1, num_y_axis=2 , figsize=(8, 7))
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 295} id="aWZ2Y_qgD0Wj" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739595885, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 589, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="f5c20613-df45-43a1-b70c-f55c863a3c5c"
# Classement des points lignes en fonction de leur contribution au 1er axe
# Le paramètre de la méthode plot_row_contrib indique que c'est pour l'axe numéro 1 que les contributions sont ici
# représentées
my_mca.plot_row_contrib(num_axis=1, nb_values=10, figsize=(10, 4))
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 295} id="GnoRRZx_D0ZM" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739601308, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 611, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="3dbe067f-3c7c-4ca2-f17f-72d5fd32d43b"
# Classement des points lignes en fonction de leur cos2 sur le 1er axe
my_mca.plot_row_cos2(num_axis=1, nb_values=10, figsize=(10, 4))
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 295} id="EB7qjLFCEaUC" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739605339, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 296, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="4b0ca2e8-8cdb-4284-f920-818cae1762d6"
# Classement des points colonnes en fonction de leur contribution au 1er axe
my_mca.plot_col_contrib(num_axis=1, nb_values=10, figsize=(10, 4))
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 295} id="FGFvEcPkEaWy" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739612156, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 693, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="39127baf-d14f-4c50-a2b4-7edda15c26b7"
# Classement des points colonnes en fonction de leur cos2 sur le 1er axe
my_mca.plot_col_cos2(num_axis=1, nb_values=10, figsize=(10, 4))
```

<!-- #region id="iwStGmGuE7lT" -->
#### **Prince**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="I3aJtlrHEaZS"
X = df
import prince
mca = prince.MCA(
     n_components=2,
     n_iter=3,
     copy=True,
     check_input=True,
     engine='auto',
     random_state=42
)
mca = mca.fit(X)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="nbUB0ADyEacC" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739937773, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 174, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="6da34da8-1bcb-4573-d4cd-0f5d0a0fec03"
print(
    'Valeurs propres :',mca.eigenvalues_,'\n',
    'Total de l inertie à expliquer :',mca.total_inertia_,'\n',
    'Inertie expliqué :',mca.explained_inertia_
)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 791} id="XVB3BTmkEafA" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1634739807755, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 1163, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "photoUrl": "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/default-user=s64", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="f6211de7-c687-4267-aeeb-0f627084819a"
ax = mca.plot_coordinates(
    X=X,
    ax=None,
    figsize=(6, 6),
    show_row_points=True,
    row_points_size=10,
    show_row_labels=False,
    show_column_points=True,
    column_points_size=30,
    show_column_labels=False,
    legend_n_cols=1
)
ax.get_figure()#.savefig('images/mca_coordinates.svg'
```

column_coordinates(X)
```

mca.row_coordinates(X)
```

<!-- #region id="8Ihx16uIGZWw" -->
### **Analyse Factorielle des Données Mixtes  (FAMD)**
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="nDaMMgWJkHpx" -->
L'**analyse factorielle des données mixtes** (AFD)  
est une méthode de réduction des dimensions des variables comprenant des données quantitatives et qualitatives mixtes en un nombre réduit de composantes pour des raisons de synthèse de l'information.   
Cette analyse peut être définie par l'opérations matricielles: **FAMD = PCA+ MCA**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 403} id="fSil-NvCFbEW" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860225654, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 438, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="365060a1-7e76-466c-94aa-c9b204914b25"
import pandas as pd

X = pd.DataFrame(
    data=[
        ['A', 'A', 'A', 2, 5, 7, 6, 3, 6, 7],
        ['A', 'A', 'A', 4, 4, 4, 2, 4, 4, 3],
        ['B', 'A', 'B', 5, 2, 1, 1, 7, 1, 1],
        ['B', 'A', 'B', 7, 2, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2],
        ['B', 'B', 'B', 3, 5, 6, 5, 2, 6, 6],
        ['B', 'B', 'A', 3, 5, 4, 5, 1, 7, 5]
    ],
    columns=['E1 fruity', 'E1 woody', 'E1 coffee',
             'E2 red fruit', 'E2 roasted', 'E2 vanillin', 'E2 woody',
             'E3 fruity', 'E3 butter', 'E3 woody'],
    index=['Wine {}'.format(i+1) for i in range(6)]
)
X['Oak type'] = [1, 2, 2, 2, 1, 1]
X
```

<!-- #region id="rR07tOYnHEDc" -->
#### **Prince**
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="_6aUrQONEHaO"
famd = prince.FAMD(
    n_components=3,
    n_iter=5,
    copy=True,
    check_input=True,
    random_state=42,
    engine="sklearn"
)
famd = famd.fit(X.drop('Oak type', axis='columns'))
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 174} id="prvP7RjbE-p3" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860229727, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 7, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="a6fbf083-387e-4563-c7a5-34dc8f75b054"
famd.eigenvalues_summary
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="orNMwCd-Hn4S" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860240025, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 203, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="c52943a2-0016-41a2-8e48-a11e7f852bc5"
print("Inertie total du model: \n",famd.total_inertia_,'\n')
print("Valeurs Propres associées aux Dimensions: \n",famd.eigenvalues_,'\n')
print("% d'inertie expliqué pour chaque Dimension: \n",famd.percentage_of_variance_,'\n')
print("% d'inertie cumulative expliquén: \n",famd.cumulative_percentage_of_variance_)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 364} id="WEs8dB4DaUcy" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860243446, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 11, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="b451af12-6835-4c8f-d96e-dc1c27b0e21c"
famd.scree_plot()
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 237} id="qXUNa0qIFbJW" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860246745, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 217, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="6af50e3f-0a1c-4c78-8dc5-b4326c78c7f8"
famd.row_coordinates(X)
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 488} id="0etUEBcgFYHK" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860387230, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 1462, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="218b4ed8-6fb5-43d3-c065-79577de828bb"
def row_cordinate_plot(data,cord1, cord2 , famd , hue ):
    plot_array = pd.concat([famd.row_coordinates(data), data[hue] ], axis=1)
    return plot_array

plot_array = row_cordinate_plot(X,0, 1 , famd ,  "Oak type")
ax = sns.scatterplot(data=plot_array, x=0, y=1, hue="Oak type" , palette="deep")
plt.title('row cordinate plot')
# Set x-axis label
plt.xlabel('Coordonnée 0\n('+str(round(famd.percentage_of_variance_[0],2)) +'%)' )

# Set y-axis label
plt.ylabel('Coordonnée 1\n('+str(round(famd.percentage_of_variance_[1],2)) +'%)' )

def label_point(x, y, val, ax):
    a = pd.concat({'x': x, 'y': y}, axis=1)
    a["val"]= val
    for i, point in a.iterrows():
        ax.text(point['x']+.02, point['y'], str(point['val']))

label_point(plot_array[0], plot_array[1], X.index, ax )
```

```python id="qfugG_jqF3jz" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860480142, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 10, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="933be7f7-c584-475f-92b5-4cbe17c6aad3" colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 394}
famd.column_contributions_
```

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 488} id="hu7Bdn6K7oPW" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1680860377327, "user_tz": -120, "elapsed": 1159, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="6bb0ff11-e2a6-4560-80fc-757d9982d2a2"

plot_array = famd.column_contributions_
ax = sns.scatterplot(data=plot_array, x=0, y=1 , palette="deep")
plt.title('column contributions plot')
# Set x-axis label
plt.xlabel('Coordonnée 0\n('+str(round(famd.percentage_of_variance_[0],2)) +'%)' )

# Set y-axis label
plt.ylabel('Coordonnée 1\n('+str(round(famd.percentage_of_variance_[1],2)) +'%)' )

def label_point(x, y, val, ax):
    a = pd.concat({'x': x, 'y': y}, axis=1)
    a["val"]= val
    for i, point in a.iterrows():
        ax.text(point['x']+.002, point['y'], str(point['val']))

label_point(plot_array[0], plot_array[1], plot_array.index, ax )
```

<!-- #region id="6rv_UDUN69Th" -->
###Analyse Procustéenne Généralisée (GPA)
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="ngztuY15AzLm" -->
Méthode mathématique qui permet de réaliser des transformations sur des tableaux multidimensionnels de manière à réduire la distance euclidienne entre ces tableaux.

L'analyse procrustéenne généralisée est une méthode qui permet de trouver la transformation optimale qui aligne deux ou plusieurs ensembles de données. Cette transformation peut inclure un changement d'échelle, une translation, une rotation ou une réflexion.
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="pC_CBRDDFTMo" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1701762650490, "user_tz": -60, "elapsed": 6, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 112} outputId="ac373c96-5993-4386-9064-7cd7b33db818"
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
import pandas as pd
# Chargement des données IRIS
iris = load_iris()
iris_data = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
iris_data.head(2)
```

```python id="od5NCVMTFbRn" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1701762608015, "user_tz": -60, "elapsed": 6, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def analyse_procuste_generalisee(df, epsilon=1e-4):
    """
    Applique l'analyse de Procuste généralisée à un dataframe.

    Args:
        df: Le dataframe à analyser.
        epsilon: La valeur limite de l'écart entre la moyenne et le spécimen de référence.

    Returns:
        Le dataframe transformé.
    """

    # Choisissez un spécimen de référence défini arbitrairement (ligne random).
    ref_idx = np.random.randint(0, df.shape[0])
    ref = df.iloc[ref_idx, :]

    # Utilisez l'analyse procustéenne pour aligner chacun des spécimens sur la référence.
    for i in range(df.shape[0]):
        if i == ref_idx:
            continue
        df.iloc[i, :] = procrustee(df.iloc[i, :], ref)

    # Calculez la moyenne de la population ainsi modifiée.
    mean = df.mean(axis=0)

    # Si l'écart entre cette moyenne et le spécimen de référence est plus grand qu'une certaine valeur, choisissez l'individu moyen comme référence et reprenez à l'étape 2/
    while np.linalg.norm(mean - ref) > epsilon:
        ref = mean
        for i in range(df.shape[0]):
            if i == ref_idx:
                continue
            df.iloc[i, :] = procrustee(df.iloc[i, :], ref)
        mean = df.mean(axis=0)

    return df

def procrustee(v1, v2):
    """
    Effectue une transformation de Procrustée d'un vecteur v1 sur un vecteur v2.

    Args:
        v1: Le vecteur à transformer.
        v2: Le vecteur de référence.

    Returns:
        Le vecteur transformé.
    """

    # Centre les vecteurs.
    v1 -= v1.mean()
    v2 -= v2.mean()

    # Calculez la matrice de rotation.
    R = np.dot(v2, v1.T) / np.linalg.norm(v2)**2

    # Appliquez la matrice de rotation.
    return np.dot(R, v1)
```

```python id="mtoxqeUIzmNm" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1701762620692, "user_tz": -60, "elapsed": 10808, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}}
# Appliquez l'analyse de Procuste généralisée.
iris_data_transforme = analyse_procuste_generalisee(iris_data)
```

iris_data_transforme

<!-- #region id="RQZVBjPt1lb1" -->
#Reduction de dimension
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 112} id="Kjc5ZMns1oeN" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1701697627644, "user_tz": -60, "elapsed": 11, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="a73573ab-0156-46d0-d50c-228870efeb74"
# Avec seaborn
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd

iris  = sns.load_dataset("iris")
iris.head(2)
```

<!-- #region id="NyJxEB4v2fZT" -->
## PCA
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="nO26NWIR6CtF" -->
Méthode de réduction de la dimensionnalité linéaire.
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="flZsN77l1yRF"
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA

# Réaliser la PCA
pca = PCA(n_components=2)
pca.fit(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])

# Récupérer les données réduites
reduced_data = pca.transform(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])
```

<!-- #region id="YmpvmY4_2oHq" -->
##Isomap
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="p-_gX5QI6AQO" -->
 Méthode de réduction de la dimensionnalité non linéaire. Cela signifie qu'il tente de réduire la dimension d'un ensemble de données tout en préservant les relations non linéaires entre les points de données.
<!-- #endregion -->

```python colab={"base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"} id="txNDe1tN2jKT" executionInfo={"status": "ok", "timestamp": 1701693815457, "user_tz": -60, "elapsed": 416, "user": {"displayName": "Florian H.", "userId": "05323848775778397032"}} outputId="2df46e69-6e94-4904-c479-2100c82d71da"
from sklearn.manifold import Isomap

# Réaliser l'Isomap
isomap = Isomap(n_neighbors=10, n_components=2)
isomap.fit(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])

# Récupérer les données réduites
reduced_data = isomap.transform(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])
```

<!-- #region id="nev295oB2yVz" -->
##LLE
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="f-XusC3o50a-" -->
 Méthode non linéaire similaire à Isomap. Elle utilise également les distances géodésiques pour calculer la proximité entre les points de données.
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="VY_WHvoc2vWi"
from sklearn.manifold import LocallyLinearEmbedding

# Réaliser l'LLE
lle = LocallyLinearEmbedding(n_neighbors=10, n_components=2)
lle.fit(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])

# Récupérer les données réduites
reduced_data = lle.transform(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])
```

<!-- #region id="zlQpRmNH2_La" -->
## MDS
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="qzvjqHdH534B" -->
Méthode non linéaire qui préserve la similarité entre les points de données.
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="-DvPa_ca22O0"
from sklearn.manifold import MDS

# Réaliser MDS
MDS = MDS(n_components=2, normalized_stress='auto')

# Récupérer les données réduites
reduced_data = MDS.fit(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])
```

<!-- #region id="_a20Evy53MAN" -->
##t-SNE
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="cBJoarfh57Q9" -->
Méthode non linéaire qui préserve la similarité entre les points de données, même si ces points de données sont répartis de manière non uniforme.
<!-- #endregion -->

```python id="F_S13ZtU3GAq"
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE

# Réaliser le t-SNE
tsne = TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=30, n_iter=1000)

# Récupérer les données réduites
reduced_data = tsne.fit_transform(iris.iloc[:, 0:4])
```
